summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 8 warning, 11 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:100 — `writeGateArtifacts()` called without try/catch in `runGateInline`. `cmdGate` (gate.mjs:286) wraps the equivalent path. Add matching error protection for parity.

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:100 — `writeGateArtifacts()` called without try/catch in `runGateInline`; disk I/O failure would propagate unhandled. `cmdGate` wraps the equivalent path in a catch. Add matching error protection for parity.

🟡 `SPEC.md:26` — AC10 calls for "integration test with a Jest JUnit reporter" but tests use inline XML strings. Functionally equivalent but doesn't match spec letter. Backlog: add true e2e test or amend spec.

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:100` — `writeGateArtifacts()` lacks try/catch, unlike the equivalent path in `cmdGate` (gate.mjs:286). Disk I/O failure would crash instead of degrading gracefully. Backlog: wrap in try/catch for parity.
🟡 test/worktree.test.mjs:282 — No test for `runGateInline` with unsafe taskId (path traversal). Only tested through `cmdGate`. Add a direct test since the return shapes differ.
🟡 test/worktree.test.mjs:282 — No test verifies `runGateInline` persists gate results to `STATE.json` gates array (run.mjs:114-139). Silent regression risk for dashboard visibility.

🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:69` — `buildConfigOverride` checks `outputFile` for `..` but not path separators. Defense relies on `path.join` behavior (safe, but fragile if ever changed to `path.resolve`). Should match `isUnsafeTaskId` rigor.
🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:289` — Internal error catch correctly emits `verdict: "FAIL"` (fail-closed). The `ok: false` distinction from normal flow is actually good design.
| Dead code | PASS | Previous 🔴 (unused imports) resolved. No dead functions/variables/branches remain. |
